title: Bake headless Chromium into the Hermes sandbox base, browse via L7 proxy with strict NSS CA trust
rationale: Sandboxes had agent-browser + Playwright code but no Chromium binary because Dockerfile.base dropped upstream's playwright install; runtime download fails since the only egress is the L7 MITM proxy. Bake it in the base (rarely-changing, all sandboxes) and wire proxy routing + NSS CA import at runtime in start.sh, gated fail-safe so it can never break the gateway launch.

description: no response in chat after switching inference provider (anthropic) and re-onboarding. Gateway /health was 200 the whole time so it looked healthy, but real completions failed. Root cause: the OpenShell inference ROUTE drifted out of sync with the agent model. reonboard-clean.sh (NEMOCLAW_PROVIDER=build) reuses the running gateway and updates the agent config model but does NOT reset the inference route, so it stayed on anthropic-prod/claude-sonnet-4-6 while the agent asked for nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b giving HTTP 400 no compatible inference route available. The earlier anthropic attempt gave HTTP 403 connection not allowed by policy. Diagnose with nemoclaw inference get (authoritative active route); the agent config.yaml model can look correct while the route is wrong. recover was required because the running Hermes gateway cached the old inference bundle at startup, and it worked even though health was already 200. Never docker exec curl to test (netns returns 000); use host port 8642.
resolution: Run nemoclaw inference set --provider nvidia-prod --model nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b --sandbox hermes to reset+validate the route, then nemoclaw hermes recover to make the running Hermes gateway re-fetch the inference bundle. Verify with a real completion from host 127.0.0.1:8642.
